page.title: Night Squid Fishing Charter in Marbella

data.meta_description: Book a night squid fishing charter in Marbella from Puerto Banús. 2-hour trips from €749, skipper, drinks, and gear included. Catch your dinner under the stars.
data.summary: Night squid fishing charters in Marbella offer a unique 2-hour experience from €749, departing from Puerto Banús. All charters include a licensed skipper, fishing gear, drinks, and snacks, with the catch cleaned and bagged for you.
key_facts: 2-hour night squid fishing trips depart from Puerto Banús.
Prices start at €749 for up to 9 passengers (Astondoa 40).
Includes licensed skipper, fishing gear, bait, and all drinks.
Catch your own squid – crew clean and bag them for you.
Trips run after sunset; best season is June–October.
No experience needed – crew provide full instruction.
Squid fishing is calm, family-friendly, and suitable for all ages.

faq.q: What time do night squid fishing charters start?
faq.a: Most trips depart around 20:00–21:00, depending on sunset. The 2-hour charter runs until 22:00–23:00. You can also book a longer 4-hour charter that includes squid fishing plus a coastal cruise.

faq.q: Do I need a fishing license for squid fishing in Marbella?
faq.a: No. The charter includes a licensed skipper who holds the necessary permits. You just turn up and fish. The gear, bait, and instruction are all provided.

faq.q: What kind of boat is used for squid fishing?
faq.a: We use our Astondoa 40 (12.5m, up to 9 passengers) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, up to 11 passengers). Both are comfortable motor yachts with shaded cockpits, perfect for night fishing.

faq.q: Can I eat the squid I catch?
faq.a: Absolutely. The crew clean and bag your catch. Many guests take it back to their villa or apartment to cook. Some restaurants in Puerto Banús will also prepare your catch for a small fee.

faq.q: Is squid fishing suitable for children?
faq.a: Yes. Squid fishing is a gentle, low-effort activity. Kids as young as 5 can join. The crew provide simple jigging rods and show them how to catch squid. Life jackets are available for all ages.

faq.q: What is included in the price?
faq.a: The price includes a licensed skipper, fuel, fishing gear and bait, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Gratuities are not included.

faq.q: What should I bring for a night squid fishing trip?
faq.a: Wear comfortable clothing and a light jacket (evenings can be cool offshore). Non-slip shoes are recommended. Bring a camera – the sunset over La Concha is spectacular. We provide everything else.

faq.q: Can I book a private night squid fishing charter?
faq.a: Yes. All our charters are private – you and your group only. No strangers on board. You choose the departure time and duration. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange a custom trip.

data.body_html:
Experience the thrill of squid fishing night charter Marbella aboard a private motor yacht from Puerto Banús. For €749 (2 hours, up to 9 guests), you get a licensed skipper, all fishing gear, drinks, snacks, and your catch cleaned and bagged. No experience needed – just turn up and fish under the stars.

Why Choose a Night Squid Fishing Charter?

Squid fishing is one of the most relaxing and rewarding activities on the Costa del Sol. You don’t need strength or skill – just patience and a steady hand. The crew show you exactly how to jig the rods. Within minutes, you’ll feel the tug of a squid. It’s a perfect family-friendly adventure, suitable for ages 5 to 85.

Night fishing also means cooler temperatures, fewer crowds on the water, and spectacular views of Marbella’s coastline lit up at night. The silhouette of La Concha mountain against the twilight sky is unforgettable.

Our Fleet for Squid Fishing

We use two comfortable motor yachts for night squid fishing trips, both departing from Puerto Banús:

BoatLengthMax GuestsPrice (2 hours)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749
Azimut 3912.5m11€749

Both boats feature a shaded cockpit, onboard toilet, and ample seating. The price includes a licensed skipper, fuel, fishing gear, bait, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%).

What to Expect on Your Night Squid Fishing Trip

  • Departure: Puerto Banús, usually 20:00–21:00.
  • Duration: 2 hours (longer charters available on request).
  • Fishing: Crew provide rods, jigs, and bait. They teach you the jigging technique.
  • Catch: Typically European squid (Loligo vulgaris). The crew clean and bag them.
  • Relaxation: Enjoy drinks and snacks while you wait for bites.
  • Return: Back at Puerto Banús with your fresh catch.

The best season for squid fishing is June through October, when waters are warm and squid are abundant. However, we run trips year-round, weather permitting.

Why Book with Boat Rental Marbella?

We are a local operator based in Puerto Banús with over a decade of experience. Our skippers know the best fishing spots – from the lee of Cabopino to the reefs off Estepona. You get a private charter, not a crowded group trip. All our boats are well-maintained and fully insured. Read more about our boat charter services in Marbella.

How to Book Your Night Squid Fishing Charter

Booking is simple. Choose your preferred boat (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39), select a date, and send us a WhatsApp message. We’ll confirm availability and arrange the departure time. Payment is via bank transfer or card. Cancellations up to 48 hours before departure receive a full refund.

For groups larger than 11, or if you want a longer charter, we can arrange a larger vessel from our fleet – such as the Mangusta 80 – but note that squid fishing is best suited to the smaller, more nimble boats.

Frequently Asked Questions

What time do night squid fishing charters start?

Most trips depart around 20:00–21:00, depending on sunset. The 2-hour charter runs until 22:00–23:00. You can also book a longer 4-hour charter that includes squid fishing plus a coastal cruise.

Do I need a fishing license for squid fishing in Marbella?

No. The charter includes a licensed skipper who holds the necessary permits. You just turn up and fish. The gear, bait, and instruction are all provided.

What kind of boat is used for squid fishing?

We use our Astondoa 40 (12.5m, up to 9 passengers) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, up to 11 passengers). Both are comfortable motor yachts with shaded cockpits, perfect for night fishing.

Can I eat the squid I catch?

Absolutely. The crew clean and bag your catch. Many guests take it back to their villa or apartment to cook. Some restaurants in Puerto Banús will also prepare your catch for a small fee.

Is squid fishing suitable for children?

Yes. Squid fishing is a gentle, low-effort activity. Kids as young as 5 can join. The crew provide simple jigging rods and show them how to catch squid. Life jackets are available for all ages.

What is included in the price?

The price includes a licensed skipper, fuel, fishing gear and bait, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Gratuities are not included.

What should I bring for a night squid fishing trip?

Wear comfortable clothing and a light jacket (evenings can be cool offshore). Non-slip shoes are recommended. Bring a camera – the sunset over La Concha is spectacular. We provide everything else.

Can I book a private night squid fishing charter?

Yes. All our charters are private – you and your group only. No strangers on board. You choose the departure time and duration. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange a custom trip.

Book Your Night Squid Fishing Adventure

Ready to catch your own dinner on the Costa del Sol? Book a squid fishing night charter Marbella today. Contact us via WhatsApp or use the booking form on our site. We’ll have you on the water within 24 hours. Don’t forget to check our other experiences for more unique ways to enjoy Marbella’s coastline.
